etymology.description: 𢆶 (silk threads; little things) + 戍 (to guard against) = watching over the fine details -> meaning related to examination or cautious attention
etymology.interpretation: The traditional character 幾 combines the ideas of fine, intricate details as represented by 𢆶 (silk threads) with the vigilance implied by 戍 (to guard against), emphasizing careful observation or examination. This reflects the care needed when dealing with small objects or tasks, originally extending to the notion of small tables or subtle quantities.
etymology.simplification: As a standalone character, 几 simplified 幾 by omitting components, retaining the essence of smallness in its form.

examples.chinese: 这个几很适合放在角落(這個几很適合放在角落)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège jī hěn shìhé fàng zài jiǎoluò.
examples.english: This small table is perfect for placing in the corner.

examples.chinese: 他用几来支撑他的书本(他用几來支撐他的書本)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng jī lái zhīchēng tā de shūběn.
examples.english: He uses the small table to support his books.

examples.chinese: 几上的花瓶非常精美(几上的花瓶非常精美)。
examples.pinyin: Jī shàng de huāpíng fēicháng jīngměi.
examples.english: The vase on the small table is very exquisite.
